transcript.clean_text: stage seven of la welter a magnificent day one you'd reach for the bike but not when you saw the profile 17 climbs in total just six of them categorized and category one tests bookending the day so straight out of the back door it was to be a mighty test that surely would establish a breakaway would roglich hand over the red jersey the big question so how would the breakaway establish itself surely on that first test and so it proved once the flag dropped we were almost immediately climbing well six riders went off up the road including three from dsm who were really setting their stall out on the chase though and on the first major descent quite technical suddenly we had 29 riders contending for a breakaway win well the gap went up to around about the five minute mark throughout the day the question was what would be the response of jumbo visma and primus roglich it was a lot to be contended with trenton was setting the pace an infernal one but suddenly movistar decided that they were going to roll the dice kara pass from india's grenadiers was monitoring the situation this was a big move until this valverde just catching a snag in the road and out he went of the race i'm afraid shoulder damage for him and at 41 years of age you'll be wondering whether indeed it is the end of the road hobbling not only this race but any chance at the world championships as well we hope to see valverde back but he's no longer part of this race well that shock really amended the profile of the day in terms of what was achievable by those gunning from the red jersey palliative meanwhile out front this sivikov pushed on out of the breakaway shipped a chain and taking advantage straight away on the attack was storer storer had not been taking too many turns out front because his teammate roman bardet was in the main group of escapees so store versus sivakov would that be the party kron and verona decided that they were going to reach out and join the fun well with the last major test of the day still up ahead of them with ramps of 14 and 16 percent who would survive stora had started amongst the favorites at the beginning of the day if it was to be a breakaway and sure enough he set off and looked magnificent verona we thought may have a chance to catch him but it wasn't to be sivakov started to fade as well and storer was bringing it home for dsm and of course for himself this win has been a long time coming for his team joy for the man from perth he delivered well throughout the day jan palance had been in the virtual lead and several others from the the big greyhound group of 29 gross heart across the line and wondered if he would be in red as polance's efforts failed and faded so did those of some other big names lander was in trouble so was guillaume martin yates came home leading a group of favourites including mass megalanga lopez burnout and of course rowlich himself in the end it was enough for roglish to stay in red but some big gaps for others and an extended one i'm afraid fernanda storer dominant on the day a beautiful performance from the man down under who came out on top verona sivikov cuss haig baday gross hartner who missed out on taking the jersey in the end by a modest margin of just eight seconds great performance by adam yates he looks strong roglic looks in control unflappable you might say but eight seconds is not a huge margin and there's still two movie star riders in the frame mass and lopez polance hovering bernal hague cuss blasoff and now yates at a minute and 22. so what about stage eight we're heading for the big lagoon the manga del mar it'll finish on a promontory but as a relief there is virtually no climbing on the day a couple of kickers and indeed a technical finale should be one for the sprinters certainly want to look forward to this has proven to be a race that bites and always provides an amazing story we race on atlabuelta you
